topics.title: Scan a receipt
topics.summary: Open Expenses → tap + → Scan Receipt. Point the camera at your receipt — Yoley's document scanner detects edges and corrects perspective automatically. Once captured, it reads the amount, VAT, supplier and date on-device (no internet needed) and lets you review before saving.
questions: How do I scan a receipt?
How do I add a receipt by taking a photo?
Can I scan receipts offline?
How does receipt scanning work in Yoley?
topics.body_markdown:
## Steps

1. Tap the **Expenses** tab → **+**.
2. Choose **Scan Receipt** to open the document scanner.
3. Hold your phone over the receipt — the scanner highlights the document outline automatically.
4. Tap the shutter (or let the scanner capture automatically when it's happy with the frame).
5. Review the scanned image; tap **Retake** if needed, then confirm.
6. Yoley reads the receipt on-device and fills in the **Amount**, **VAT**, **Supplier** and **Date** fields for you.
7. Check the pre-filled details, adjust any field if needed, pick a category, then tap **Save**.

> **Tip:** Scanning works entirely on your device — no internet connection is required and your receipt images are never sent to a server.

> **Note:** If the scanner can't confidently detect a total from a label (e.g. "Total" or "Grand Total"), it falls back to the largest monetary figure on the receipt. Always double-check the **Amount** field before saving.

topics.title: How do I recommend Yoley to a friend?
topics.summary: We're working on a proper referral programme and plan to build a "Refer a friend" link into the app soon. In the meantime, the easiest way is to point them at Yoley on the Apple App Store.
questions: How do I recommend to a friend?
How do I refer a mate to Yoley?
Is there a Yoley referral programme?
How do I tell someone about Yoley?
Can I invite a friend to Yoley?
topics.body_markdown:
A referral programme is on the roadmap — we're planning to add a dedicated "Refer a friend" link inside the app to help grow the Yoley community. It's not live yet.

Until then, the easiest way is to share Yoley on the **Apple App Store** — just search for "Yoley" or send the App Store link from your share sheet. Word of mouth from another tradesperson is still the best recommendation a new user can get.

> **Note:** When the referral programme launches we'll reach out to everyone using Yoley — so keep your email address up to date in **Settings → Business Profile**.

topics.title: Record a verbal (phone) approval
topics.summary: Open the quote, tap Manually Approve, choose Phone, add a note like "Spoke to John at 2pm", then tap Confirm Approval. This logs the approval with a timestamp.
questions: Can I record a verbal approval by phone?
How do I record a phone approval?
How do I log a spoken yes from the customer?
topics.body_markdown: Follow the standard manual approval flow and pick **Phone** as the method. Add a short note describing the call — who you spoke to, when, and any specifics they confirmed. The timestamp is recorded automatically.

topics.title: Record an approval by text or WhatsApp
topics.summary: Open the quote, tap Manually Approve, choose Text, add a note (you can paste the customer's message), then tap Confirm Approval.
questions: Can I record approval given by text or WhatsApp?
How do I log a WhatsApp approval?
How do I record an SMS acceptance?
topics.body_markdown: Follow the standard manual approval flow and pick **Text** as the method. Paste the customer's message into the note field if you want a record of the exact wording, then tap **Confirm Approval**.

topics.title: Quote comments carry through
topics.summary: Yes. Any comment added to a quote — including the note you add during manual approval — is automatically carried through as notes on the job when scheduled and on the invoice when converted.
questions: Do quote comments carry through to the invoice?
Does the comment I add when approving a quote carry into the job?
Are quote notes copied to the invoice?
topics.body_markdown:
Comments and notes on a quote flow through two places:

- **Into the job** — when you schedule a job from an accepted quote, the approval comment is pre-filled as the job note.
- **Into the invoice** — when you convert an accepted quote to an invoice, the quote's notes copy across so the customer sees the same information.

You can always edit or remove them before sending.

topics.title: Convert a quote to an invoice
topics.summary: Open an Accepted quote and tap Convert to Invoice. Line items, customer details and notes copy across automatically. Set a due date (your default payment terms apply) and tap Send Invoice. The invoice arrives with a Pay Now link.
questions: How do I convert a quote to an invoice?
Can I turn an accepted quote into an invoice?
How do I invoice a finished job from its quote?
topics.body_markdown:
1. Open an `status:accepted` Accepted quote.
2. Tap **Convert to Invoice** at the bottom of the screen.
3. All line items, customer details and notes copy across automatically.
4. Set a **due date** (your default payment terms apply, but you can override).
5. Tap **Send Invoice**.

The customer receives the invoice with a Pay Now link so they can pay by card immediately. Funds settle to your bank account within 1–2 working days.

topics.title: Create a quote
topics.summary: Tap the Quotes tab → + → pick or add a customer → enter a job title → add line items (including Kit Bundles) → tap Save to keep as a draft or Send to deliver by email or SMS.
questions: How do I create a quote?
How do I make a new quote?
How do I start a quote?
How do I add a quote?
How do I create and send a quote?
topics.body_markdown:
1. Tap the **Quotes** tab → **+** (top right).
2. Search for an existing customer or tap **New Customer** to add one on the fly.
3. Enter a job title (e.g. "Full bathroom refit"). Add a reference number if needed.
4. Tap **Add Line Item** to add labour, materials, or use a **Kit Bundle**.
5. Optionally add notes, photos or a validity expiry date.
6. Tap **Save** to keep as a draft, or **Send** to deliver immediately.

> **Tip:** Kit Bundles let you save common job packages (e.g. "Standard boiler service") and add them with a single tap. Set up bundles in **Settings → Kit Bundles**.

Your customer gets a branded quote by email or SMS they can accept with one tap. The whole process takes under 30 seconds once your customer and Kit Bundles are set up.

topics.title: Kit Bundles
topics.summary: Kit Bundles are pre-saved groups of line items for jobs you do regularly — for example a "Standard boiler service" that includes 1 hour labour + service kit + travel. Add the whole bundle to a quote with one tap. Set up bundles in Settings → Kit Bundles.
questions: What are Kit Bundles?
What are kit bundles?
How do kit bundles work?
Where do I set up Kit Bundles?
topics.body_markdown:
Kit Bundles save time on jobs you quote for repeatedly. Instead of entering each line item manually, bundle them into a named package and add the whole thing in one tap.

Set up bundles in **Settings → Kit Bundles**. Each bundle can mix labour, materials and travel lines. When you add a bundle to a quote you can still edit individual lines — the bundle is a starting point, not a lock.

topics.title: Manually approve a quote
topics.summary: Open the quote and tap the green Manually Approve button next to the status badge. Pick how the customer confirmed (Phone, Text, Email or Other), add an optional comment, then tap Confirm Approval. The comment carries through as a note on the scheduled job.
questions: How does manual quote approval work?
Can I approve a quote on behalf of a customer?
How do I record a customer's approval manually?
How do I mark a quote as accepted myself?
topics.body_markdown:
When a customer approves over the phone, via text or by any other method not captured in the app, you can record the approval manually.

1. Open the quote from the Quotes list.
2. Tap the green **Manually Approve** button next to the status badge at the top of the screen.
3. Select how the customer approved: **Phone**, **Text**, **Email**, or **Other**.
4. Optionally add a comment (e.g. "Agreed to proceed, start date 15th").
5. Tap **Confirm Approval**.

> **Tip:** The comment you add during manual approval is automatically carried through as a note on the job when you schedule it.

topics.title: Send a quote to a customer
topics.summary: Open the quote and tap Send. Choose Email, SMS or Share Link. The customer receives a branded quote they can accept with one tap.
questions: How do I send a quote to a customer?
How do I email a quote?
Can I send a quote by text?
How do I share a quote link?
topics.body_markdown:
From any quote, tap **Send**. Choose to deliver by:

- **Email** — sends a formatted email with a link to the branded quote page.
- **SMS** — sends a short message with the quote link.
- **Share Link** — copies the link so you can share via WhatsApp or any other channel.

The customer sees a professional-looking quote with your branding and a one-tap accept button.

topics.title: Quote statuses
topics.summary: Draft = saved, not sent. Sent = delivered to the customer. Opened = customer has opened the link. Accepted = customer confirmed (digitally or via manual approval). Declined = customer said no. Expired = past the validity date without response.
questions: What do the quote statuses mean?
What do the different quote statuses mean?
What does Opened mean on a quote?
What does Accepted mean on a quote?
topics.body_markdown:
| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `status:draft` Draft | Saved but not yet sent to the customer. |
| `status:sent` Sent | Delivered to the customer via email or SMS. |
| `status:opened` Opened | Customer has opened the quote link. |
| `status:accepted` Accepted | Customer has accepted — ready to schedule and invoice. |
| `status:declined` Declined | Customer declined the quote. |
| `status:expired` Expired | Past the validity date without response. |

topics.title: Can I share my Yoley login?
topics.summary: Your Yoley account is tied to one device at a time to keep your data secure. If you need several people to see the same data and coordinate work, upgrade to Yoley Max for multi-user access. Every plan lets you send your trading data to a nominated accountant.
questions: Can I share my account login?
Can two people use the same Yoley account?
Can my partner log in at the same time?
Is Yoley multi-user?
Can my team share an account?
topics.body_markdown:
Sharing a single login isn't supported — Yoley only allows one device to be signed in at any moment, and signing in on a new device will sign you out of the old one.

If you run a team, **Yoley Max** is the answer. Max adds multi-user access so several people can see the same jobs, quotes and invoices and coordinate their work.

> **Tip:** Whatever plan you're on, you can share your trading data directly with your accountant — they don't need a Yoley login to receive it.

topics.title: How secure is my data?
topics.summary: Your data is stored in encrypted form on your phone and backed up to your iCloud account — both of which are inherently secure. Nothing sensitive is stored on Yoley's own servers.
questions: How secure is my data?
Is my Yoley data encrypted?
Where is my data stored?
Do you keep my data on your servers?
Is my customer information safe?
topics.body_markdown:
Yoley stores your data locally on your device in an encrypted form, and syncs it to **your** iCloud account via Apple's CloudKit. That means:

- Your customer records, quotes, invoices and receipts never sit on a Yoley server.
- Backups are encrypted and scoped to your Apple ID, just like your photos and Notes.
- Only you (and people you explicitly share an account or data export with) can access it.

> **Tip:** Turn on Face ID or a PIN in **Settings → Security** for an extra layer of protection if your phone gets left unlocked.

topics.title: What happens if I lose my phone?
topics.summary: Your data is encrypted on the phone and backed up to your iCloud account, so it's safe. Sign into Yoley on a new device with the same Apple ID and your data restores from the iCloud backup automatically.
questions: What happens if I lose my phone?
How do I recover my data on a new phone?
Is my Yoley data backed up?
Can I restore Yoley on a replacement phone?
What if my phone is stolen?
topics.body_markdown:
Because Yoley encrypts your data locally and syncs it to **your** iCloud account, a lost or stolen phone doesn't put your data at risk.

## To restore on a new phone

1. Sign in to your new iPhone with the same Apple ID.
2. Install Yoley from the App Store.
3. Open Yoley — it'll pull your data down from iCloud automatically.

## A small note on sync timing

Yoley syncs to iCloud roughly every 5 minutes. So if you worked on something in the minutes just before losing the phone, a small amount of the very latest work could be missing when you restore.

> **Tip:** If your phone is stolen rather than lost, also use **Find My** on another Apple device to lock or erase it remotely. That doesn't affect your iCloud backup — your Yoley data will still restore onto your replacement handset.

topics.title: Can I get the Yoley source code?
topics.summary: The Yoley app is designed with security at the forefront of our thinking — the code and your data are both protected. So no, we're definitely not sharing that… but nice try!
questions: Can you send me the source code for Yoley?
Can I have the Yoley source code?
Is Yoley open source?
Can I see the code behind Yoley?
Do you publish the Yoley codebase?
topics.body_markdown:
Yoley is a proprietary product. We keep the codebase private to protect both our tradespeople's data and the integrity of the platform.

If you're a developer curious about how we've built Yoley, we'd love to hear from you — drop us a line at help@yoley.app.
